---
title: "Projects"
---

Below are the active and completed research projects from the Pichugin Lab. Code repositories for each project are hosted in our [GitHub organization](https://github.com/Pichugin-Lab).

---

## Active Projects

### Multilevel Selection Dynamics

We are developing a general mathematical framework for multilevel selection that accounts for group formation, reproduction, and dissolution. The goal is to identify conditions under which group-level selection can drive the evolution of cooperation and novel collective-level traits.

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
**Status:** Active  
**Collaborators:** [Names]  
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
[{{< fa brands github >}} Repository](https://github.com/Pichugin-Lab){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-dark}
[{{< fa file-lines >}} Preprint](#){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-primary}
:::
:::

---

### Eco-Evolutionary Feedback Models

This project studies how ecological interactions (competition, predation, mutualism) feed back into evolutionary dynamics and vice versa. We build and analyse ordinary and stochastic differential equation models to understand long-term evolutionary trajectories.

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
**Status:** Active  
**Collaborators:** [Names]  
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
[{{< fa brands github >}} Repository](https://github.com/Pichugin-Lab){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-dark}
:::
:::

---

## Completed Projects

### Evolution of Life-History Traits

A theoretical investigation of the trade-offs between growth rate, fecundity, and survival that shape life-history strategies across biological taxa. Published results are available via the [Publications](publications.qmd) page.

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
**Status:** Completed  
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
[{{< fa link >}} Publication](#){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-primary}
[{{< fa brands github >}} Repository](https://github.com/Pichugin-Lab){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-dark}
:::
:::

---

## Linking External Repositories

This lab website is designed to link directly to other repositories within the [Pichugin-Lab GitHub organization](https://github.com/Pichugin-Lab). As new repositories are made public they will be featured here with descriptions, documentation links, and usage examples.
